title: NASA working to streamline development of nuclear electric propulsion demo mission
summary: NASA is implementing a streamlined management approach to accelerate the development of a nuclear electric propulsion demonstration mission, aiming for a launch within two and a half years.

title: China builds institutional framework for space computing push
summary: China is creating an industrial policy framework to support the development of space-based computing infrastructure, establishing new coordinating bodies to guide this initiative.

title: SpaceX to raise at least $75 billion in IPO
summary: SpaceX plans to raise at least $75 billion in its IPO, valuing the company at over $1.75 trillion. This marks one of the largest public offerings in corporate history.

title: NASA declares end to MAVEN Mars mission
summary: NASA has officially ended the MAVEN Mars mission after losing contact with the spacecraft for six months. The investigation into the cause of the mission's demise is still ongoing.

title: AST SpaceMobile sees New Glenn setback delaying initial commercial service into 2027
summary: AST SpaceMobile's initial commercial service for its direct-to-smartphone constellation is delayed into the first half of 2027 due to Blue Origin's New Glenn launchpad explosion. The setback is expected to push the timeline by three to six months.

title: After cooperation on SMILE mission, ESA and China chart parallel but separate paths
summary: ESA and China have launched the joint SMILE magnetosphere mission after a decade of cooperation, but future collaborations remain uncertain despite shared goals.

title: Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers
summary: Murata is evaluating Xona's satellite timing service to enhance backup capabilities for GPS-dependent systems in telecom and data centers. Xona is building a low Earth orbit network to support these applications.

title: Europe is rearming together — except in space
summary: Europe is strengthening its security measures in response to Russian aggression and concerns about U.S. commitment, but space defense remains underdeveloped compared to other sectors. The continent is prioritizing broader rearmament efforts while lagging in space security initiatives.

title: Muon Space unveils Starship-class satellite platform for orbital data centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a Starship-class satellite platform tailored for orbital data centers, with its first launch planned for 2028 after securing initial customers.

title: Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end
summary: Blue Origin aims to resume New Glenn launches by the end of the year after assessing that damage to its launch pad is less severe than initially feared. The company's CEO confirmed the optimistic outlook for the rocket's return to flight.

title: A rainbow patchwork quilt shows agriculture from space | Space photo of the day for June 4, 2026
summary: A composite image from NASA's latest Earth-observing mission showcases a vibrant patchwork of agricultural fields in South Africa. The rainbow-colored quilt highlights the diversity and scale of farming activities visible from space.

title: 'Silo' season 3 trailer teases showing us how the world ended (video)
summary: The trailer for 'Silo' season 3 hints at revealing how the world ended, suggesting a new world is imminent. The show's narrative takes a dramatic turn with this revelation.

title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is not an alien spacecraft: SETI hunt for 'technosignatures' comes up empty
summary: Scientists conducted a SETI search for 'technosignatures' on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS but found no radio signals, confirming it is a natural object rather than an artificial alien spacecraft.

title: Meteorite found in Sahara desert may be 1st evidence of lost solar system world
summary: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara Desert provides evidence of a lost moon-size world that existed shortly after the solar system formed. This discovery could offer new insights into the early solar system.

title: Aurora alert! 4 Earth-bound CMEs could spark northern lights as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight
summary: Multiple Earth-bound coronal mass ejections (CMEs) may trigger strong geomagnetic storms and auroras as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight. Aurora chasers are encouraged to watch for potential northern lights displays.

title: Can black holes turn into white holes? It's not such a crazy idea, scientists say
summary: New research suggests that primordial black holes from the Big Bang could live long enough to transform into white holes, which emit energy. This challenges previous estimates of black hole lifespans.

title: 'The mirror passed with flying colors': NASA just took its last look at the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope before launch
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's primary mirror and are preparing to ship it to Florida for launch. The telescope has passed its latest tests with high marks.

title: Our choice for the best deep-space viewing telescope — now with $200 off, this telescope deal is worth grabbing fast
summary: The Celestron NexStar 8SE telescope is currently discounted by $200 during an early Amazon Prime Day deal, making it the best price of the year. An expert tester praised the telescope's performance in a recent review.

title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
summary: An astronaut aboard Artemis II captured a striking photo of Earth from deep space, highlighting the planet's beauty and its cosmic context. This image underscores the mission's role in advancing human space exploration.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4908-4912: Goodbye Campo Marte, It’s Been Fun!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover completed its 47th successful drill at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, marking a significant milestone in its mission. The rover's team utilized the drilling downtime to conduct additional scientific activities.

title: NASA Finds New Way Earth May Have Received Elements Needed for Life
summary: NASA-supported scientists have discovered a new mechanism by which early Earth may have received essential elements for life. The study also proposes a revised role for Jupiter in distributing these elements across the young solar system.

title: International Sea Level Satellite Observes El Niño Precursor
summary: The international Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich satellite observed a warm Kelvin wave, a precursor to El Niño, moving from the western Pacific to South America's coast in early 2026. This data highlights the satellite's role in monitoring ocean conditions and climate patterns.

title: Journey to the Center of the Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA/ESA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88) as it journeys through the Virgo Cluster. Located 63 million light-years away, M88 is an active galaxy with a dynamic center.

title: NASA Drains 66-Million-Gallon Reservoir to Upgrade Critical Water System
summary: NASA drained a 66-million-gallon reservoir at Stennis Space Center to upgrade its High Pressure Industrial Water Facility, which supports rocket engine tests. The reservoir was pumped down to its lowest level since the 1960s.

title: NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission, which studied Mars' atmosphere for over 11 years, has concluded after an unexpected loss of signal. The spacecraft operated far beyond its original one-year mission timeline.

title: NASA Space Roboticist Challenge
summary: NASA is launching a robotic arm with seven degrees of freedom to low Earth orbit as part of the Fly Foundational Robots (FFR) mission. Access to the robotic arm is being opened to select U.S. researchers for experiments.

title: Look Up!
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronaut Jack Hathaway monitored the automated docking of SpaceX's Dragon cargo spacecraft to the ISS. The event occurred 259 miles above Earth as part of Expedition 74.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage from an anomaly and identifying future danger areas. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. The mission faces uncertainties regarding timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36 (LC-36) with the goal of returning the facility to flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different entities. The launches highlight the growing competition and activity in the global space industry.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early universe phenomena.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, focusing on rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar projects.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo project among the highlights.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enables European research institutes to participate in space science missions. The 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway is a recent success supported by this programme.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young minds, test and develop materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for the agency's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance training, operations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is being used to create advanced simulation environments and improve accessibility to space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission successfully tested its parachute system in a high-temperature environment, simulating re-entry conditions. This marks a critical step in ensuring safe landings for future spacecraft.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars landing, undergoing rigorous sterilization to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever to fly on Mars, will slow the rover during its six-minute descent in 2028.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx spacecraft on May 19, evaluating parachute performance and controlled landing dynamics. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to assess the capsule's Earth re-entry systems.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a scheduled July launch.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times larger than its predecessor Condor-XL. This new bus is designed to cater to the emerging orbital-data-center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to return to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move follows industry feedback shaping the agency's future approach.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of this year after assessing launch pad damage. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight commitment on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are focused on accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space programs.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) program, aiming to deliver missile defense capabilities by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space will acquire Astrobotic to expand its lunar infrastructure capabilities. The deal aims to support NASA's Artemis program by providing end-to-end lunar surface solutions.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised $500 million in a Series D funding round to continue its current trajectory without expanding into new business lines or major investments like a factory. The company's President and COO emphasized the focus on maintaining its existing path.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace is partnering with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a VLEO demo satellite, with a planned launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named 'Ultra' aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for delivery in 2028.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company, operating in the aerospace and defense sector, began trading on Wednesday.

title: Assessing SpaceX Finances, Addressable Market, and the AI Pitch Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO next week, and Via Satellite has analyzed the company's financial filings and market potential. Leading space analysts discuss key insights ahead of the public offering.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised $500 million in a Series D funding round led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. The company is focused on advancing its space technology and mission capabilities.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, replacing Eric Jensen who will now serve as COO. Stevens joins from ASRC Federal.

title: MDA Space to Supply Antennas to BAE Systems for Epoch 2 Missile Warning Constellation
summary: MDA Space has been selected by BAE Systems to supply antennas for the Epoch 2 missile warning constellation for the U.S. Space Force. The subcontract involves designing and delivering the antennas as part of the constellation's development.

title: Juniper Research Expects Massive Spike in Direct-to-Cell Subscribers
summary: Juniper Research predicts a significant increase in direct-to-cell (D2C) subscribers, with over 130 million active monthly users expected by 2031. The forecast is detailed in their report titled 'Direct to Cell Market 2026 – 2031.'

title: Are Orbital Data Centers the Next Frontier of AI Infrastructure?
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers to support AI infrastructure, with competition among hyperscalers in cloud, AI, and space sectors. This trend is seen as a potential next major development in space technology.

title: Sighting the Dark Fleet: Hyperspectral’s Role in Securing Safer Seas
summary: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation to strikes by the U.S. and Israel, triggering a regional war. Hyperspectral technology is highlighted for its role in securing maritime operations during the crisis.
